response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with a strong background in web platforms, low-latency services, and developer tooling, but his profile has no overlap with the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His high-performance work at Dunnhumby (low-latency HTTP microservices) and embedded/robotic experience at Altom are adjacent to systems programming but are categorically different from the hardware-software co-design and silicon-level performance analysis this role requires.
strengths: 14+ years of software engineering experience across multiple domains, satisfying the educational/experience threshold for Bachelor's path
Demonstrated low-latency and high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby) shows performance-oriented mindset
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks indicates awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or silicon-level performance analysis — the core technical requirement of the role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization
Security and export-control screening suitability is unclear; no mention of relevant clearances or citizenship documentation

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No GPU or AI accelerator domain knowledge
